summary: Strong jams on Good Lovin', Hard To Handle; historic campus vibe
blurb: The show features punchy renditions of Good Lovin', Hard To Handle and a memorable China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue. Jerry’s rare pedal‑steel interlude and Pigpen’s organ add unique flavor. Recording quality is rough, but the performance shines for early‑70s Deadheads.
review:
On May 3 1970 the Dead took the stage at Foss Hill, Wesleyan University, after a politically charged campus atmosphere. The set bursts open with a gritty Hard To Handle that rolls straight into an inspired China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider pairing, followed by two powerful Good Lovin' jams bookending a tight drum break. Jerry Garcia slipped in a surprise pedal‑steel cameo during the New Riders interlude and later delivered his usual guitar fire, while Ron "Pigpen" McKernan’s organ and harmonica lift the middle of the set, especially on Turn On Your Lovelight. Though the surviving tapes are audience recordings riddled with din and muffling, listeners can still hear the band’s chemistry and the historic energy of a campus concert in an era of protest.

The recording landscape is uneven: the Cotsman tape (gd70-05-03.aud.cotsman.9378.sbeok.shnf) offers the most complete picture despite its “rough” character, while other audience captures are nearly unlistenable. For those willing to brave the mud, this show rewards with strong jams and a rare glimpse of Jerry’s pedal‑steel experiment, making it a worthwhile slice of early‑70s Dead history.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings note strong jams and memorable moments (Good Lovin', China Cat Sunflower, pedal‑steel) while the third is neutral; overall sentiment leans modestly positive about the performance despite poor audio quality.

best_recording.reason: Most complete audience tape; captures the strongest jams despite rough sound, making it the clearest window onto the performance.

key_highlights: Punchy Hard To Handle opening and two solid Good Lovin' renditions
Rare Jerry pedal‑steel interlude during the NRPS set
Pigpen’s organ/harmonica shines on Turn On Your Lovelight
Historic campus atmosphere with political announcements adds flavor

band_performance.Jerry: Adds a surprising pedal‑steel solo during the NRPS set and delivers solid lead work on the main set, though tape mud obscures some nuance.
